original_tweet: AI will replace 80% of jobs in the next 5 years. If you're not learning to code or prompt engineer right now, you're going to be left behind.

analysis.tone: confident and performative with aggressive urgency
analysis.assumptions: that coding/prompt engineering are the only paths to future relevance, and that AI job displacement will be uniform across industries
analysis.angle: disagree and provoke with an unexpected angle
analysis.why: A philosophical challenge to the narrow tech-centric worldview will spark deeper debate about human value beyond technical skills.

replies.reply: Learning to code is just learning to speak to the new boss. The real question is: will you own the company, or just be the best employee?
AI will replace the people who make predictions like this first. The future belongs to those who learn to be human, not machines.
AI isn't taking your job. Your job was just a cage you built for yourself. The real question is what you'll do when the door is finally kicked open.
AI won't replace jobs. It will replace people who think like machines. Your value isn't in learning its language, but in remembering your own.
The 80% being replaced aren't jobs. They're tasks. Learning to code a machine is the machine's victory lap. The real skill? Becoming the 1% it can't replicate: a human with a soul.
AI will replace the job. But who has the job? Discover the one who watches the fear of being left behind. That watcher was never employed.
AI won't replace jobs. It will replace job descriptions. The skill that matters isn't coding—it's knowing what to ask for. That can't be engineered.
Learning to code for AI is like learning to shovel for the bulldozer. The guy who drives the bulldozer is learning to garden.
